cadence: five AI insight posts per day; one targeted documented research post plus four rotating research facts across 8bit, NHS, BYA, and ADB

items.theme: Mistral AI public AI hiring signal
items.asset_brief: Small source-backed stat card: role count, top tags, sample roles, operator question.

sample_titles: Applied AI, Forward Deployed Machine Learning Engineer, Critical and Sovereign Institutions, EMEA

items.funnel: AI Dev Board data, Not Human Search agent-readiness check, or 8bit research follow-up

x.copy:
Mistral AI has 82 open AI developer roles indexed on AI Dev Board. The current cluster is llm, pytorch, fine-tuning, and infrastructure. Avg published salary: $265k.

Their agent-facing profile is thinner: mistral.ai scores 40/100 on Not Human Search, with llms.txt and a structured API present.

https://aidevboard.com/company/mistral-ai
x.approved_final_copy:
Mistral AI has 82 open AI developer roles indexed on AI Dev Board. The current cluster is llm, pytorch, fine-tuning, and infrastructure. Avg published salary: $265k.

Their agent-facing profile is thinner: mistral.ai scores 40/100 on Not Human Search, with llms.txt and a structured API present.

https://aidevboard.com/company/mistral-ai

linkedin.copy:
Looking at @Mistral AI through the public hiring data:

1. 82 indexed AI/ML roles in AI Dev Board.
2. Current sample tags cluster around llm, pytorch, fine-tuning, infrastructure.
3. Sample roles include ML Engineer - Pre-Training; Applied AI, Forward Deployed Machine Learning Engineer, Critical and Sovereign Institutions, EMEA; Research Engineer, Data Infrastructure.
4. Avg published salary in the indexed sample: $265,000.

The useful read is where the operational work is moving: open model deployment, pre-training, applied ML, and enterprise rollout.

Mistral AI is a useful public lens on this area. Does the next bottleneck sit in model access, internal integration, or agent-facing docs?

NHS follow-up lens: public agent-readiness profile shows llms.txt, structured API. That is useful for checking what agents can inspect without a human browsing the site.

Data:
https://aidevboard.com/company/mistral-ai
https://nothumansearch.ai/site/mistral.ai
linkedin.approved_final_copy:
Mistral AI has 82 indexed AI/ML roles in AI Dev Board.

The current hiring sample clusters around LLMs, infrastructure, PyTorch, distributed systems, and fine-tuning. The average published salary in the indexed sample is $265,000.

Sample roles:
1. ML Engineer - Pre-Training.
2. Applied AI, Forward Deployed Machine Learning Engineer, Critical and Sovereign Institutions, EMEA.
3. Research Engineer, Data Infrastructure.

The useful read is where the operating work is sitting: pre-training, applied ML, data infrastructure, and enterprise deployment.

The agent-readiness profile is thinner. Not Human Search sees llms.txt and a structured API, but no OpenAPI spec or ai-plugin manifest.

Hiring data: https://aidevboard.com/company/mistral-ai
Agent-readiness profile: https://nothumansearch.ai/site/mistral.ai
linkedin.posting_note: Posted from verified Shane Cheek / Founder at 8bitconcepts LinkedIn profile via supervised one-shot browser recovery after direct Voyager cookie sources returned 302 and page-context Voyager returned 401.

quality_gate: target company or influential person is named/tagged naturally but never as the first token
documented sources are attached
uses live ADB or NHS stats
does not negatively call out the target
includes a useful operator question or recommendation
flags longform paper opportunity when the stat is large enough

items.asset_brief: Cost stack: data, integration, evals, observability, maintenance, model fees.

x.copy:
Little fact from the 8bit research stack: model API fees are usually 10-20% of the AI build cost. The rest is data plumbing, integration, evals, observability, and maintenance.

Source: https://8bitconcepts.com/research/the-integration-tax.html

linkedin.copy:
Little fact from the 8bit research stack: model API fees are usually 10-20% of an AI build. The bigger lines are data pipelines, system integration, evaluation, observability, and maintenance.

That is why the budget conversation has to happen before the model-choice conversation.

Source:
https://8bitconcepts.com/research/the-integration-tax.html
linkedin.approved_final_copy:
Model API fees are usually 10-20% of an AI build.

The bigger costs are the work around the model: data pipelines, system integration, evaluation and testing, observability, and maintenance.

That is why the budget conversation has to happen before the model-choice conversation.

Source:
https://8bitconcepts.com/research/the-integration-tax.html
linkedin.posting_note: Posted from verified Shane Cheek / Founder at 8bitconcepts LinkedIn profile via LinkedIn Voyager API; immediate script verification missed the live share URL, then public curl verification matched the canonical activity page.

quality_gate: one small verified fact
source-backed by 8bit research or case-study page
routes to the relevant product or paper
no sales-first CTA
materially different fact_key from previous posts

x.copy:
Little fact from the NHS MCP audit: developer tools had 1,686 indexed sites. Health had 72. Education had 28. The agent ecosystem is crowded in tools and thin in regulated verticals.

Source: https://8bitconcepts.com/research/q2-2026-mcp-ecosystem-health.html

linkedin.copy:
Little fact from the NHS MCP audit: developer tools had 1,686 indexed sites. Health had 72. Education had 28.

The agent ecosystem is crowded around tools and still thin in regulated verticals where the workflow value is high.

Source:
https://8bitconcepts.com/research/q2-2026-mcp-ecosystem-health.html
linkedin.blocker: LinkedIn auth could not reach a verified posting identity: direct cookie Voyager rejected chrome-profile-3 with 302, page-context Voyager returned 401, and supervised browser recovery stayed on LinkedIn Login 'We’re signing you in' for Shane Cheek h*****@8bitconcepts.com instead of reaching the feed/composer. No public post was created.
linkedin.approved_final_copy:
One split from the Not Human Search MCP audit:

Developer tools: 1,708 indexed sites, 23.9% of the index.
Health / medical: 70 sites, 1.0%.
Education: 28 sites, 0.4%.

The agent ecosystem is much denser around developer tooling than the verticals where workflow constraints are heavier.

Source:
https://8bitconcepts.com/research/q2-2026-mcp-ecosystem-health.html

items.asset_brief: Privacy boundary: remote discovery, local movement.

x.copy:
Little fact from the BYA case study: the remote MCP surface has 4 tools and accepts no harness files, GitHub handles, generated memories, API keys, or file contents. The actual move runs locally.

Source: https://8bitconcepts.com/case-studies.html#bringyour

linkedin.copy:
Little fact from the BYA case study: the remote MCP surface has 4 tools and accepts no harness files, GitHub handles, generated memories, API keys, or file contents.

The product boundary is the point: discovery can happen through an agent surface, but private context moves locally.

Source:
https://8bitconcepts.com/case-studies.html#bringyour

x.copy:
Little fact from the ADB skill data: agents appeared in 2,437 indexed roles, but distributed-systems paid higher on average: $252,318 vs $229,919.

Source: https://8bitconcepts.com/research/q2-2026-ai-compensation-by-skill.html

linkedin.copy:
Little fact from the ADB skill data: agents appeared in 2,437 indexed roles, but distributed-systems paid higher on average: $252,318 vs $229,919.

Volume and negotiating leverage are not the same signal.

Source:
https://8bitconcepts.com/research/q2-2026-ai-compensation-by-skill.html
